messages.content:
## Chat Conversation
*   (/mnt/shared-storage-user/yuanfei/yileitu/conda_env/deepseek) root@yileitu-pg9xn-924111-worker-0:/mnt/shared-storage-user/yuanfei/yileitu# git clone --recursive **git@github.com**:deepseek-ai/DeepGEMM.git Cloning into 'DeepGEMM'... **git@github.com**: Permission denied (publickey). fatal: Could not read from remote repository. Please make sure you have the correct access rights and the repository exists.

You’re trying to clone via **SSH** (`git@github.com:...`), but this machine/container doesn’t have a usable SSH key registered with GitHub, so GitHub rejects it (`Permission denied (publickey)`).

### Option A (simplest): clone via HTTPS instead of SSH
```bash
git clone --recursive https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepGEMM.git
```
If you already ran part of the clone or want to ensure submodules are fetched:
```bash
cd DeepGEMM
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

### Option B: keep using SSH (set up keys)
1) Check for an existing key:
```bash
ls -la ~/.ssh
```

2) If none, create one:
```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
```

3) Add the public key to GitHub (**Settings → SSH and GPG keys**):
```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

4) Test:
```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
```

5) Retry:
```bash
git clone --recursive git@github.com:deepseek-ai/DeepGEMM.git
```

### Common gotcha in containers/HPC
If you’re inside a container or remote worker, your usual `~/.ssh` keys may not be present. In that case, HTTPS cloning is usually fastest, or you need to mount/forward your SSH keys into the environment.

If you tell me whether you prefer HTTPS or SSH and whether this is a container/cluster job, I can give the exact commands for your setup.
